author: nero_eth
content:
Yeah exaclty, in 8037, state gas is an economic mechanism, not a computational one. The reservoir lets you pay more for state growth without consuming more parallelizable resources, because regular gas already covers the execution cost of state-creating operations.
So, regular gas meters all resources that benefit from parallelization while state gas only meters the long-term cost of growing the state.

Regarding 2, hard to tell right now. The goal is to have the fork done in summer, which would imply that the sepolia forks would happen in early/mid summer. This will depend a lot on when the CL-side is ready - we might be finished earlier on the EL, which is great because this would mean we have the final numbers for repricings earlier and more time for the ecosystem to learn what it concretely means for their apps and adopt.
cc <@467031582146822144>
